rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
Rough-cut drafts for articles.
Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
Always 'you', never 'the student'; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Connections-paragraph carve-out (2026-05-03) applies: line 28 'The room harvests everyone's before the lecture names the pattern.' is a bare room-read sentence as its own paragraph after the Connections question — in-scope-by-design per check_writing.md rule 7 and check_pedagogy.md rule 27. Not flagged.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
Watch out for planting jargon too early.
Audit the closer when trimming a body-prose forcing function.
No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
Earn every technical term on first use.
No narrating the student's past or present state
rules_evaluated.evidence: 59: 'You built a plan you trust without running it, and you ran a second-pass walk-down on something small enough that the pairing *felt* worth the 15 minutes.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Closer pat-on-head re-narrating completed activity; flip agency to artifact or move — e.g. 'The pairing ran: human read, push-back, agent walk-down. Module 3 takes the same move into durable infrastructure.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No overclaim of student verification or coverage
Golden path only in the body.
Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module.
Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 63: '## Pre-reads before Module 3' — non-canonical section whose body directs the student to read; header noun-phrase, lacks a command verb.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rename to '## Read before Module 3' to lead with a verb; body already structures the reads imperatively.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use the target's title, never its filename.
LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow.
Hard-grep phrase list (zero-tolerance, run before any student-facing ship)
